concept_candidates.label: Town
concept_candidates.description: A human settlement larger than a village but smaller than a city, typically governed by municipal authorities and serving as a local hub for commerce, education, and culture.
concept_candidates.justification: The candidate represents a reusable, semantically stable geographic concept that is general enough to be used across multiple documents. It aligns with the grounding summary and does not duplicate existing ontology classes.

concept_candidates.label: Neighborhood
concept_candidates.description: A geographically localized community within a larger town, city, suburb or rural area, often comprising a single street and its buildings.
concept_candidates.justification: Neighborhood is a stable, general concept that can be reused across documents to model localized communities, fitting ontology-oriented modeling better than treating it as a document-specific mention.

ontology_relation_candidates.label: builtBy
ontology_relation_candidates.description: Relates an object or structure to the person or organization that constructed, assembled, or otherwise brought it into existence.
ontology_relation_candidates.justification: The relation "built by" is a stable, reusable semantic link that appears across many documents and is not a duplicate of existing ontology properties. It aligns with the Wikidata property P1071 and is widely applicable to constructions and artifacts.
